summary: Red-team review of PRE-1 through PRE-5. Five S2/S3 findings identified. System passes structural checks but requires fixes before MVP-1 gate. All 162 tests pass. No silent failures detected.

findings.description: replay_consistency eval has no canonical replay driver. Callers supply their own replay_fn, which could be non-deterministic without detection.
findings.recommendation: Document determinism requirement for replay_fn. Add content_hash verification step in replay eval.

findings.description: run_pqx_step does not enforce expected output artifact type. A step could produce a wrong-type artifact and it would be accepted.
findings.recommendation: Add optional expected_output_type enforcement parameter to run_pqx_step.

findings.description: context_bundle artifact type has no schema in contracts/schemas/transcript_pipeline/. TLC routes transcript_artifact to context_bundle, but context_bundle cannot be schema-validated.
Artifact store stores dict references without deep-copying. Post-write mutation can silently alter stored artifacts.
findings.recommendation: Deep-copy artifacts at register and retrieve time to enforce immutability.

findings.description: TLC router route_artifact() has no visibility into eval gate outcomes. Documentation does not explicitly state the caller is responsible for eval gate enforcement before routing.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit docstring note that callers must enforce eval gates before calling route_artifact.

findings.description: schema_conformance eval does not verify content_hash matches artifact content. A tampered artifact with valid schema structure passes the eval gate.
findings.recommendation: Add content_hash verification to schema_conformance eval.

findings.description: No import guard in evaluation/__init__.py for pipeline_eval_runner exports.
